reason: The mockup perfectly addresses the design brief by providing an intuitive and highly structured interface for configuring AI system prompts and variables. It establishes a strong visual hierarchy, clearly grouping the main editor, dynamic token actions, and helpful guidance cards (Gestalt principle of proximity). Information density is well within Miller's Law limits with only 5 main functional sections, keeping cognitive load low. Colors conform to WCAG 2.1 AA standards with robust contrast ratios on text and active targets, and JetBrains Mono font-family successfully distinguishes technical variables from prose.
concerns: The 2px border on the central text area editor is visually heavy compared to the 1px borders of surrounding cards, which might draw the user's focus away from the prompt content itself.

While a tip block suggests testing, the interface lacks an integrated real-time preview or dry-run playground on the same screen, requiring the user to navigate to another tab or section to see the prompt's qualifying behavior in action.
The tap-to-insert variable buttons could benefit from more pronounced hover and active state transitions to strengthen the sense of interactivity and prevent user uncertainty when using touch input.
